dialogos.texto_en: Deep-fried chicken, spinach with sesame sauce,\nsimmered eggplant, boiled tofu, stew, curry...
I'd say that's about right.
What are you doing?\nCooking such a large amount of food at this time.
Oh, welcome back, Miku.
I'm planning to send some food to Futaro.\nI'm about to pack it now.
Oh, I remember you bought a food sealer.\nYour intended use for it was sending meals to Futaro, right?
(Futaro's been in Tokyo for a few months.\n In the beginning,\n I was also busy with my new life.)
(But now, I've grown accustomed to the\n vocational school where Miku and I enrolled,\n and I have more free time at hand.)
(That's why I can spend my holidays\n doing things I like%p-2;――%p;)
That's it!\nThank you for helping me pack, Miku.
Sure, but sending him food like this....\nI mean you're acting like his mother.
That's okay.\nFutaro isn't used to cooking yet,\nthus he can use its taste as a reference for his own cooking.
And by sending him food like this,\nhe'll remember me, every time he has a meal.
For me it looks like as if you're taming him, huh?
Stop phrasing it like that, would you?\nI'm supporting Futaro in my own way.
But it's really a lot.
Well, even I feel a little overwhelmed...\nBut that's what you get if you want to balance the nutrients.
Oh, right.\nI should include a note on food combinations.\nFutaro might eat without thinking about nutrient balance.
It's possible to arrange things in order from the top.
He may have bad taste and that's why\nhe doesn't really care about what kind of food he's eating.
Now, I hope he becomes more aware of his eating habits.
You seem more lively, Nino.\nYou're really like a mother.
(I'm lively, am I?)
(I was worried about myself\n when I was separated from Futaro%p-2;――%p;)
(I'll get less lonely if I'm worrying about his eating \n habits. I'm doing it out of love too.)
(Should I secretly go to Tokyo to see him\n and have him tasting it in front of me?)
Nino?\nWhy are you suddenly grinning like that?
Huh? Was I grinning?
Yeah, you looked like you were up to something devious.\nPoor Futaro.
Wh-why would you think that?
Because, you were probably thinking\nabout secretly going to see him, right?
I wasn't thinking about that...
Nino, you're always easy to read\nwhen it comes to Futaro.
Ooh...
But, maybe it's okay.
If Futaro receives such delicious food,\nhe'll definitely want to see you, Nino.
R-really?\nYeah, that must be it!
This is like taming an animal with food.
Ugh!\nYou always have to put a twist on things!
(Finally meeting after a long time of not seeing each \n other, I wonder how Futaro will react?)
(Will he be surprised and happy,\n or scold me for coming without permission?)
(Even if he scolds me,\n I won't give up. After all%p-2;――%p;)
(Love shines when one's on the offensive.\n Right, Futaro?!)
